back_translation: And the king was shaken, and he went up to the upper room of the gate and wept; and thus he said as he walked, 'My son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom — who will give my death — I in your place — Absalom, my son, my son!'
And it was told to Joab: 'Behold, the king is weeping and mourning over Absalom.'
And the victory was on that day for mourning to all the people, because the people heard on that day, 'The king is grieving over his son.'
And the people stole into the city on that day, as people who are humiliated steal away in their fleeing in battle.
And the king covered his face, and the king cried out with a great voice, 'My son Absalom, Absalom, my son, my son!'

And Joab came to the king in the house and said, 'You have shamed today the faces of all your servants, who saved your life today, and the lives of your sons and your daughters, and the lives of your wives, and the lives of your concubines —'

by loving those who hate you and hating those who love you. For you have declared today that you have no commanders or servants — for I know today that if Absalom were alive and all of us today were dead, then it would be right in your eyes.

And now, arise, go out and speak to the heart of your servants. For by the LORD I swear: if you do not go out, no man will lodge with you tonight; and this evil will be worse to you than all the evil that has come upon you from your youth until now.'

And the king arose and sat in the gate. And to all the people they told, saying, 'Behold, the king is sitting in the gate.' And all the people came before the king. And Israel had fled, each man to his tents.

And all the people were disputing in all the tribes of Israel, saying, 'The king delivered us from the hand of our enemies, and he rescued us from the hand of the Philistines; and now he has fled from the land because of Absalom.
And Absalom, whom we anointed over us, has died in battle. And now, why are you silent about restoring the king?'

And King David sent to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, saying, 'Speak to the elders of Judah, saying, Why should you be the last to restore the king to his house, when the word of all Israel has come to the king at his house?
You are my brothers; you are my bone and my flesh. And why should you be the last to restore the king?'
And to Amasa say, 'Are you not my bone and my flesh? Thus may God do to me and thus may he add, if you are not the commander of the army before me always, in place of Joab.'
And he swayed the heart of all the men of Judah as one man, and they sent to the king, 'Return, you and all your servants.'
And the king returned and came as far as the Jordan, and Judah came to Gilgal to go to meet the king, to bring the king across the Jordan.
And Shimei son of Gera, the Benjamite who was from Bahurim, hurried and went down with the men of Judah to meet King David.
And a thousand men with him from Benjamin, and Ziba the steward of the house of Saul, and his fifteen sons and his twenty servants with him; and they rushed to the Jordan before the king.
And the ferry crossed to bring over the king's household and to do what was good in his eyes; and Shimei son of Gera fell down before the king as he crossed the Jordan,

and said to the king, 'Let my lord not count it against me as iniquity, and do not remember what your servant wronged on the day my lord the king went out from Jerusalem — let the king not set it to his heart.
For your servant knows that I have sinned; and behold, I have come today first of all the house of Joseph to come down to meet my lord the king.'
And Abishai son of Zeruiah answered and said, 'Shall Shimei not be put to death for this — for he cursed the anointed of the LORD?'
And David said, 'What to me and to you, sons of Zeruiah, that you should be to me today an adversary? Today shall a man be put to death in Israel? For do I not know that today I am king over Israel?'
And the king said to Shimei, 'You shall not die.' And the king swore to him.

And Mephibosheth, the son of Saul, went down to meet the king; and he had not cared for his feet, nor had he trimmed his mustache, and his clothes he had not washed, from the day the king went away until the day he came in peace.
And when he came to Jerusalem to meet the king, the king said to him, 'Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?'
And he said, 'My lord the king, my servant deceived me. For your servant said, I will saddle for myself the donkey and ride on it and go with the king, for your servant is lame.
And he slandered your servant to my lord the king; and my lord the king is like the angel of God — do what is good in your eyes.
For all the house of my father were nothing but men of death to my lord the king; and you set your servant among those who eat at your table. What more right do I have, even to cry out to the king?'
And the king said to him, 'Why speak any more your words? I say, you and Ziba shall divide the field.'
And Mephibosheth said to the king, 'Let him take it all, after my lord the king has come in peace to his own house.'
And Barzillai the Gileadite went down from Rogelim and crossed over the Jordan with the king, to send him off across the Jordan.
And Barzillai was very old, a son of eighty years; and he had provided for the king during his stay at Mahanaim, for he was a very great man.
And the king said to Barzillai, 'You cross over with me, and I will provide for you with me in Jerusalem.'
And Barzillai said to the king, 'How many are the days of the years of my life, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?

I am a son of eighty years today. Can I know between good and evil? Can your servant taste what I eat and what I drink? Can I still hear the voice of singing men and singing women? And why should your servant still be a burden to my lord the king?
Your servant will just cross the Jordan with the king — and why should the king repay me with this reward?

Let your servant please return, that I may die in my own city near the tomb of my father and my mother. And behold, your servant Chimham — let him cross over with my lord the king, and do for him what is good in your eyes.'
And the king said, 'With me Chimham shall cross, and I will do for him what is good in your eyes; and whatever you choose upon me, I will do for you.'
And all the people crossed the Jordan, and the king crossed; and the king kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his place.
And the king crossed to Gilgal, and Chimhan crossed with him; and all the people of Judah brought the king across, and also half the people of Israel.

And behold, all the men of Israel came to the king and said to the king, 'Why did our brothers, the men of Judah, steal you away and bring the king and his household over the Jordan, and all of David's men with him?'

And all the men of Judah answered against the men of Israel, 'Because the king is near to me. And why does this anger you about this matter? Have we eaten anything from the king, or has anything been lifted up for ourselves?'

And the men of Israel answered the men of Judah and said, 'Ten shares I have in the king, and also in David I am more than you. And why have you despised me? And was not my word first for me to restore my king?' And the word of the men of Judah was fiercer than the word of the men of Israel.
